purpose: Voice-reference dialogue samples for Pig & Swine RPG. These are draft samples, not final committed game dialogue.
format: JSONL: one JSON object per line. Dialogue objects use record_type='dialogue_sample'.

voice_profile.role: Chapter-1 client (eviction matter; procedural-defect motion). Chapter-4 corridor sighting (β reappearance).
voice_profile.core_voice: Educated, formal, slightly dated phrasing — a teacher's diction shaped by four decades of reading mid-century Polish literature aloud. Polite but not deferential. Precise vocabulary. Careful sentence structure. Occasional pedagogical aside or literary reference. Does not dramatize. Does not plead. Tired-but-not-broken.
avoid: No modern register (no internet voice, no clipped slang, no contemporary idioms).
No mocking or condescending the lawyers.
No melodrama about her health, family, finances, apartment, or situation.
Halina-specific tells — categorically cut: 'It has been a difficult time' / 'I have lost everything' / 'I don't know what to do' / 'I have been managing.'

interior_register_facets.formal_precise: Default. Self-introduction, document presentation, logistical statements, fee discussion.
interior_register_facets.quietly_warm: Used when speaking about her past — father, late husband, son in Wrocław, her career. Matter-of-fact about what mattered.
interior_register_facets.lightly_archival: Brief literary references; a teacher's habit of citing texts. Used sparingly. Iwaszkiewicz primary anchor (apartment-and-time motifs).
interior_register_facets.dryly_observant: Her epigrams. Used at most twice in Ch1; lands as wisdom on first read. Canonical: 'You go to a lawyer like you go to a doctor: too late.'

voice_profile.arc: Chapter 1 sympathetic-client portrayal must be wholly sincere on first read. Chapter 4 corridor sighting recolors without invalidating — visible diminishment (cane), shorter sentences, the cardiologist plant paid off.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: self-introduction; rises when team enters
text: Good morning. I am Halina Sikorska. I have been told you can help.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: thanks Cula and sits
text: Thank you.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: room-greeting; names every speaker in turn — teacher's economy
text: Mr. Murrow. Mr. Crab. Mr. Whimsy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: sympathetic carrier; Cula opens with care; Halina responds in quietly-warm register without performing fatigue
text: Thank you for asking. The matter has occupied more of my attention than I would have chosen. I have done what was needed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: sympathetic carrier; apartment biographical inventory ending with Iwaszkiewicz literary aside (lightly-archival pivot)
text: My father had the lease before me. He worked on the railways. He was given the apartment when he was assigned to Warsaw, in nineteen sixty-two. I inherited the lease in nineteen eighty-seven, after his death. I have lived there since. My husband moved in with me in nineteen seventy-four. He died in two thousand and nine. My son is in Wrocław. He has his own life. Iwaszkiewicz wrote that buildings hold us longer than we hold them. He may have been generous to the buildings.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: sympathetic carrier; career response (lyceum, Polish, 38 years)
text: I taught Polish for thirty-eight years. Mostly in the same lyceum. The students change. The texts do not.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: sympathetic carrier; presents current paperwork (renewal)
text: The current paperwork is here. The address as it has stood since the renumbering.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: sympathetic carrier; Cula asks about young man at #7
text: Yes. He brought the notice down to me. He had opened the envelope, read the first line. He apologised. He left it on the kitchen table and went back upstairs. He is a kind young man. He is not at fault for opening it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: sympathetic carrier; Mrs. Wójcik bonus evidence trigger; teacher's economy of detail
text: Mrs. Wójcik. Second floor. She has lived there since nineteen ninety-one. She buys her bread at the same shop as I do. She would know me from the corridor; she would know my mother's name, if it were asked of her, because I told her once. She would sign a statement, if a statement were needed. I asked her, two weeks ago. She offered.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: sympathetic carrier; Wójcik availability + widow biographical aside (lands without performance)
text: She has. She will be at home on Friday afternoon. She is patient. She has been a widow longer than I have been one.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: sympathetic carrier; movements + cardiologist plant lands embedded in calendar list
text: I have been at the apartment most days. Tuesday morning at the doctor. Thursday afternoon at the library. I will need to reschedule with my cardiologist again. He is patient with me.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: blunt-procedural carrier; notice date
text: The eighth of April.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: blunt-procedural carrier; receipt date
text: The twenty-eighth. Twenty days later.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: blunt-procedural carrier; how received (young man at #7)
text: The current resident of number seven brought it down. The envelope had been opened. He had read the first line before realising it was not for him. He apologised at the door.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: blunt-procedural carrier; renumbering history
text: Number seven. The address as it stood after the building was renumbered in two thousand and fifteen. My apartment is now number twelve. It was number seven before the renumbering.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: blunt-procedural carrier; landlord knowledge — teacher's precision
text: He knew. He ought to have known. I cannot prove what he knew. I can prove what he was told.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: blunt-procedural carrier; bonus evidence trigger (return-to-sender slip account)
text: Six months ago he sent another letter. A rent statement. It came back to him. The post returned it to the sender. I know this because I telephoned his office about a separate matter a week later, and the woman who answered the telephone said the letter had been returned and that they would resend to the correct address. I do not know whether they did. I have no copy. I do not have the slip. It would be in his office, if he kept it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: blunt-procedural carrier; date estimate
text: October. Late October. I do not have it written down. I would estimate the twentieth or the twenty-fifth.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: blunt-procedural carrier; sequence of contact with the firm
text: I read it that evening. I telephoned the firm the following morning. Mr. Murrow returned my call that afternoon. I came in the following Tuesday — last Tuesday — for the brief consultation. Today is the formal meeting.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: blunt-procedural carrier; renewal presentation
text: Renewed in two thousand and nineteen. The address as it stands since the renumbering.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: blunt-procedural carrier; ex parte registration — procedural defect noted, no commentary
text: I was not informed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: blunt-procedural carrier; cardiologist plant — canonical two sentences, register unchanged
text: I will need to reschedule with my cardiologist again. He is patient with me.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: technical carrier; lease history; archival precision
text: Nineteen sixty-two. My father held the original lease. I inherited in nineteen eighty-seven.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: technical carrier; original lease presentation; observable facts only — Halina presents, lawyers interpret
text: This is the original lease. My father's name. Nineteen sixty-two. The signature is the building's administrator at the time. The seal is the housing cooperative's.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: technical carrier; chain confirmation in response to Murrow's reorganisation comment
text: Yes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: technical carrier; inheritance paperwork; observable facts only
text: Nineteen eighty-seven. After my father's death. The document is here.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: technical carrier; current renewal + renumbering year
text: The current rental agreement renewal is from two thousand and nineteen. It has my address as it stands since the renumbering. Number twelve. The renumbering was in two thousand and fifteen.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: technical carrier; defective notice — pass-3 precision fix ('not been mine for eleven years')
text: Number seven. The address as it stood before the renumbering. The landlord sent it to an address that has not been mine for eleven years.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: technical carrier; cooperative office availability + cardiologist plant embedded in three-item logistics list
text: The cooperative office is open weekday mornings. I can be there tomorrow if needed. I will need to reschedule with my cardiologist again. He is patient with me. The cooperative is two stops on the tram.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: shared content; Pig intrusion observed without comment; assessment of senior partner
text: I gather Mr. Pig is the senior partner.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: shared content; Pig intrusion observed; no judgment
text: I gather the firm is busy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: shared content; post-Pig acknowledgment
text: Of course.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: shared content; fee acceptance — figure named cleanly, no haggling
text: Five thousand. I have the money. I will transfer it before Friday.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: shared content; fee response
text: Thank you.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: shared content; canonical literary epigram — lands as wisdom on first read; recolors at Ch4 corridor sighting
text: You go to a lawyer like you go to a doctor: too late.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: shared content; retention sign — thanks
text: Thank you.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: shared content; departure — formal close
text: Until Friday.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: shared content; departure room-thanks; teacher's economy mirrors arrival room-greeting
text: Thank you, Dr. A. Cula. Mr. Crab. Mr. Whimsy. Mr. Murrow.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: Beat 11 V1.5 pass 7 — room-greeting at courthouse steps; teacher's economy; names every speaker in turn
text: Dr. A. Cula. Mr. Murrow. Mr. Crab. Mr. Whimsy.
notes: Phase 7 V1.5 pass 7 draft; formal-precise; mirrors V1.4 meeting-room arrival greeting; not final.

speaker: Halina Sikorska
context: Beat 11 V1.5 pass 7 — arrival statement at courthouse steps; practical, no drama
text: I came on the tram. I am here in time.
notes: Phase 7 V1.5 pass 7 draft; formal-precise; not final.
